any game that don't is  a no for me

as well as in steam but you can’t link that account anywhere else
this is an indie game made by a *very* small studio
so the animation is passable but it's not going to be multi-billion-dollar-corporation level, if that's what attracts you to CZN
small studios have a high chance of not getting greedy so it is really more of a good thing lol
it doesn't need to be advance, fgo comes to mind most animation is simple outside of ults
more of a 'hit and miss' since they aim at a smaller player base though, but if they 'hit' then they hit hard if you know what I mean lol
oh I hope rerolling isn't a thing here?
that needs to die in a fire
New to the game, who should I pick as the starting awakener?
in hell
nope, rerolling is not really a thing in this game
rerolling is not needed
you get way too many pull currency anyway
you can keep rerolling you first 'guaranteed' SSR until you get who you want so it is kinda built into game in a good way
all the characters in the pool are easy to get, so just pick whomever you like

I mean it can be if you really want to, but the returns are abysmal and you get barely any value out of it because the game gives you so many pulls, and the rates are so high that you're not going to have a problem pulling characters
plus just 1-2 units won't matter much cause you need alot to make a difference
that kind of rerolling I like, but the rerolling where you gotta crawl to the devs feet to get crumbs worth of pulls is what I hate
you pretty much will not find a more generous gacha on the market currently, so if you're worried about stinginess and pull income, don't be
that's a big claim, ak players and one other game name that eludes me has that claim of being generous
more than any other gacha
yeah, i've seen games with a great gameplay but that have gone complete garbage cause of greedy devs. Was not the issue in this game so far. The difficulty curve though...
limbus doesn't count since that's technically not actually gacha if you don't want to interface with it
and the rates here are better than arknights, if that's what you're using as a benchmark

- [Stats and Realm Mechanics](<https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1pnheXD_pkjz8YGmWWlQPnw4eXr6NRm-W6267c0K1_QU/edit?slide=id.p1#slide=id.p1>), by Mythag University

well the values are different; In Arknights you get 1 copy of an operator - you are done. -1 DP cost is a joke for whales. Here the dupes make way more impact, especially for late game

200 free pulls, you get to pick one of the most recent limited SSR characters for free, and they went back and retroactively buffed various one-time rewards throughout the game which adds up to approximately around another 200 pulls
isn't that bad since dupes are for whales?
Arknights income and rate is horrible if you compare it to other gacha. Especially since you need to save for 5 month+ as f2p for limited characters.
u cant name 3 gacha better lmao
but 2nd anni, never heard of this game before
the fact it has second anni means it's doing something right

one of the downsides of this game is that, in exchange for showering you in pulls, they do expect you to get at least 2-3 dupes on characters… howhever we do also get a monthly currency in the pull/sediment shop that is one free universal pull

you get 1 universal character dupe and 1 universal weapon dupe every month for free, and again the rates in this game are quite literally 2-3x better than pretty much every mainstream gacha on the market so even with dupes accounted for you generally get more than you would in most games

here you kinda have to get dupes even as a non whale lol, that's the catch. For few super core units at least. Luckly there is 1 item per month given that allows you to dupe limited units, so it is real to ascend 1-2 key units for hard content
Azur lane, Morimens, and nike. EZ
yeah it's a very small indie game so it's not particularly well known
Hell, even wuwa is technically more generous than AK lol. Also PGR.
also the limited units here are not same as in arknights - all banners get reruns and all year 2 units were recently announced to be added to standard pull

it also gets ridiculously hard as you progress which turns off a lot of the casual gacha playerbase, though 2nd anni comes with a bunch of major nerfs to story mode to make it easier to progress (this can be either a good or bad thing depending on your view)
just like most other game, so long as f2p pull smart they can thrive
weird how I never considered Arknights to be greedy even as a f2p lol. Probably cause I played since start, so I have a solid team and can just ignore uninteresting banners and only pull who I like
Maybe it's just preference. So many gacha game has that 'f2p can clear all content using basic units' for now, so I don't consider clearing everything as not greedy. It's just passable to play.
it's cos it depends. arknights lacks a lot of bs. no dupes required, no bs "standard banners"
But with bs 'limited banner'. 5 month saving for 1 character is pure greed lol.
And that is a generous calculation, assuming generous event rewards.
5 months to guarantee. u get 1/3 of a pull daily in mori for limited, 90 pulls for guarantee
Morimens way more generous event wise. Also you can always buy dupe monthly if you play it regularly.

- PvP in this game is RNG based
- A lot of strong wheel are gated by SR limited Wheel from events or SSR limited wheels from banners
- You can unlock everything by buying the special battlepass so it's kind of P2W in that regards
- There is not a lot of players doing PvP because it's mainly against the same type of meta team, previously it was stuff like Agrippa + Tawil + Gdoll + Hameln or Winkle + Cassiah + Thais + Lotan or Paralyze team or One shot AoE Helot.

Now it shifted to Clementine Bleed team
PvP isn't fun when you're mainly against the same type of shit

alright so
when u have an ultra character in your team, this will appear above their head
ok
whenever it's your turn, the first card you play will be placed into the slots
some ultra cards have special effects for being played first so read that
the swirl on the left is called "Annihilation" or something
and u can press it to get an Insight card place onto your hand and the leftmost card in the 5 slots will be deleted
after u fill up 5 slots, you can activate Ultra space or something, i forgot the name
It's basically an extra turn, and the 5 cards will be placed into your hand
the cards u stored will be carried over between fights in the dungeon so keep that in mind
